metric_definitions.label: Unit Test Pass Rate
metric_definitions.plain: Percent of BETA unit tests passing during validation
metric_definitions.target: The metric has a real baseline, a repeat run, and a clear decision rule.
metric_definitions.why: Percent of BETA unit tests passing during validation

metric_definitions.label: Code Coverage
metric_definitions.plain: Line coverage reported by the imported coverage file (Cobertura XML or coverage.py JSON).
metric_definitions.target: Higher is better; set a project floor (often 70-80 percent) and avoid regressions against the last import.
metric_definitions.why: Coverage shows how much of the code the passing tests actually exercise, so a high pass rate is not hiding untested paths.

metric_definitions.label: Test Failures
metric_definitions.plain: Number of failing tests plus errors in the imported test report.
metric_definitions.target: Lower is better; zero failing tests is the target for a green build.
metric_definitions.why: Failures point directly at what is broken right now and what to fix before the next claim of done.

metric_definitions.target: The metric has a real baseline, a repeat run, and a clear decision rule.

project_goals: Use BETA to track and improve BETA itself
Measure whether BETA is becoming more useful, reliable, actionable, and reusable across projects
Identify missing project-management, QA, data-analysis, and AI-assistant capabilities
Keep BETA usable as a no-command project manager for OMEGA and BETA itself
Track real tests, evidence reports, todos, work sessions, and AI manager output as measurable project data
Improve BETA from concrete gaps found while using it on real projects
metric_playbook.summary: BETA uses metrics for three jobs: prove the project works, prove it is improving, and decide what work deserves attention next.

decision_rules: If a critical metric regresses, stabilize it before adding new features.
If a claim is thin, collect missing evidence before advertising improvement.
If a metric improves, add a regression guard before moving on.
If effort looks wasted, verify with issue, CI, or time-log data before changing priorities.
